name: Individual Almond-Cream Tarts
recipeInstructions:
1. Whip cream and prepare tart shells by mixing flour and butter in a bowl until it resembles coarse crumbs. In another bowl, beat cold water with egg yolks, then add yolk mixture to the flour mixture, stirring well. Shape pastry into a ball.
2. Preheat oven to 400°F. Roll out pastry on a floured surface to about 1/8" thick and cut into 20 circles using a 4-1/4 inch round cookie cutter. Place each circle onto the bottom and sides of tart pans or muffin cups (cut additional circles with a 3-1/2 inch cookie cutter). Prick pastry in several places with a fork. Arrange tart pans in two jelly-roll pans for easy handling.
Bake for 20-25 minutes until lightly browned.
3. Beat egg whites and brush hot tart shells with beaten eggs. Let cool on wire racks for about 10 minutes, then loosen shells from the pans and remove them completely.
4. Prepare cream filling by mixing whipped cream and almond extract into pudding prepared according to package instructions using a total of 2 1/2 cups milk.
5. Spoon filling into tart shells and decorate with mandarin orange sections, kiwifruit, strawberries, grapes, and chocolate as desired.
6. Make glaze by heating apple jelly and water in a saucepan over medium-low heat until smooth. Brush over fruit and let cool for 10 minutes before refrigerating tarts.
